prompt:
Our correspondence chess club adjudicates games by hand and we've had three disputed rulings this season (a castling-through-check, a missed en passant pin, and a stalemate someone scored as a win). I want a rules engine that settles it, class Position in Position.java, plain Java, stdlib only.

We do NOT use FEN. Our position sheets (see positions/*.pos) are eleven lines: 'turn w|b', 'castles <rights>|-' (rights are a subset of the letters KQkq in exactly that order — K/Q white short/long, k/q black), 'enpassant <square>|-', then the eight ranks from 8 down to 1, each as the rank digit and eight cells separated by spaces — uppercase pieces white (PNBRQK), lowercase black, '.' empty. Position.parse(String) must reject with IllegalArgumentException: malformed or missing lines, ranks out of order or the wrong width, unknown cell characters, boards without exactly one king a side, pawns sitting on rank 1 or 8, a castling right whose king or rook has left its home square, and an en passant target that isn't on rank 6 (white to move) or rank 3 (black to move). render() prints the same eleven-line block back exactly (no trailing newline); turn() gives the side to move.

legalMoves() returns EVERY legal move and nothing else, as coordinate strings sorted lexicographically: 'e2e4' style, promotions with a lowercase piece letter suffix ('e7e8q', and all four of q/r/b/n are separate moves), castling written as the king's two-square step ('e1g1'/'e1c1'), en passant written as the plain capturing pawn move. The full rule set matters: sliding/knight/king/pawn moves and captures, double pushes only from the home rank, en passant only when the target square is set, promotions on reaching the last rank, castling only with the right intact, every square between king and rook empty, and neither the king's square nor the two squares it crosses attacked — and of course no move may leave your own king attacked (pins, discovered checks, kings may never stand adjacent, an en passant capture can strip a rank-5 pin and must be refused then).

apply(move) returns a NEW Position (the original must not change) and throws IllegalArgumentException for any string not in legalMoves(). It has to maintain the bookkeeping: turn flips, a double push sets the en passant target and anything else clears it, en passant removes the bypassed pawn, castling drags the rook, promotion swaps the piece, moving the king forfeits both castle rights, moving a rook off (or capturing one on) a corner forfeits that side's right.

status() returns 'checkmate' or 'stalemate' when there are no legal moves (king attacked or not), else 'check' or 'normal'.

TestMain.java pins full enumerations for club positions and — the part I trust most — move-tree node counts to depth 3 over five reference sheets; if any rule is even slightly off those totals won't reconcile. 'java TestMain.java' is the gate; tests and the positions/ sheets are read-only.
notes: 7G6 item 5: chess move-legality engine, non-FEN rank/file text format per catalog. Verification anchored EXTERNALLY: the five perft fixtures are the standard CPW suite (start 20/400/8902, kiwipete 48/2039/97862, pos3 14/191/2812, pos4 6/264/9467, pos5 44/1486/62379) and the reference engine reproduced every published count on first run, so the pinned kiwipete 48-move list is count-anchored, not self-referential. Hand-derived pins: start 20-list, check-evasion list, ep-offered list + post-capture render, ep-strips-rank-5-pin refusal list, 4-flavour promotion list + queening render/check, castling denial matrix (through-check, blocked b1, out-of-check, no-rights), rights bookkeeping renders (king move, each rook move, castle). Runtime ~0.5s. Domain check: no chess seed exists in any language; go-checkers is draughts (different rules/surface).
